source_report: ESC supply chain intelligence for the UAS/drone industry

esc_manufacturers.name: SkyStars
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Largest ESC manufacturer by product count in Forge. Budget to mid-range.

esc_manufacturers.name: T-Motor (Tiger Motors)
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Premium ESCs and motors. FLAME and VELOX series. Used in some Blue UAS-adjacent builds despite Chinese origin.
esc_manufacturers.strategic_note: T-Motor supplies ESCs to some drone integrators who claim NDAA compliance. Origin verification needed.

esc_manufacturers.name: Lumenier (GetFPV)
esc_manufacturers.ndaa_note: US company. 'NDAA' labeled products (e.g. Siege 80A NDAA). PCB assembly origin requires verification — likely allied CM, not Shenzhen.
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Largest US ESC brand. GetFPV house brand. Actively marketing NDAA-compliant products.

esc_manufacturers.name: FETtec

products.note: Sinusoidal FOC drive — smoother and more efficient than trapezoidal

esc_manufacturers.market_position: Premium German-engineered ESC. Proprietary FOC firmware (not BLHeli). First to use STM32G473 in FC/ESC. Used by high-end builders and enterprise.
esc_manufacturers.strategic_significance: European-manufactured, proprietary FOC firmware, allied supply chain. One of the few genuinely NDAA-compliant ESC options.

esc_manufacturers.name: Holybro
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Tekko32 ESC line. Major ArduPilot ecosystem supplier. DroneCAN-capable ESCs for enterprise.

esc_manufacturers.name: Hobbywing
esc_manufacturers.market_position: XRotor series. First major brand to adopt AT32F421 MCU in G2 series ESCs. Also makes enterprise-grade ESCs for large drones.
esc_manufacturers.strategic_note: Hobbywing's adoption of AT32 MCU in G2 series accelerated Chinese MCU penetration into ESC ecosystem.

esc_manufacturers.name: 603700 (Ukrainian)
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Ukrainian combat-proven ESCs. High-current designs for 7-inch and heavy-lift combat drones.

esc_manufacturers.name: KDE Direct
esc_manufacturers.market_position: US-manufactured enterprise/defense ESCs. Large drone propulsion systems. Proprietary firmware.
esc_manufacturers.strategic_significance: Genuine US-manufactured ESC for large platforms. Used in defense programs. Not FPV-class.

esc_manufacturers.name: Vertiq (IQ Motion)
esc_manufacturers.market_position: Integrated motor+ESC modules for defense/enterprise. FOC drive. Closed-loop position/velocity control.
esc_manufacturers.strategic_significance: US-designed integrated propulsion modules targeting defense market. NDAA compliant.

AM32.note: Growing. Betaflight-recommended. NDAA path via STM32 MCU.

proprietary.note: FETtec, KDE, Vertiq, Simplex — all allied manufacturers

fpv_class.gap: US brands (Lumenier, XILO) likely use Chinese CM for PCB assembly. FETtec is the only manufacturer with confirmed European assembly.

compliant_options: Holybro → not compliant despite ArduPilot ecosystem use
enterprise_class.gap: Enterprise ESCs from KDE and Vertiq are 5-10x more expensive than Chinese equivalents. Limited product range.

ndaa_esc_landscape.silicon_note: See stack-supply-chain/silicon_and_firmware.json for MCU (STM32 vs AT32) and MOSFET supply chain details — same analysis applies to standalone ESCs.
